construct.label: Somatic and ambient AI anxiety
construct.definition: Anxiety expressed as sleep disruption, bodily vigilance, background dread, avoidance, or low-specificity physiological arousal after exposure to AI capability news or demonstrations.
construct.subconstruct: sleep disruption after AI capability news

scenario_text: Imagine that shortly before bed you read credible coverage of a new AI system that can independently plan tasks, write convincing messages, and operate common software tools. The story does not say that you personally will lose work or be harmed. Still, when you try to sleep, you keep replaying the implications and feel too keyed up to settle for more than an hour.

response_scale.prompt: How anxious would this scenario make you in terms of sleep disruption or difficulty settling after AI news exposure?

scoring.missing_response_rule: Treat skipped responses as missing at the item level. Do not impute item scores for development, validation, or scoring analyses.
exclusion_criteria: Exclude respondents who fail the survey-level attention check specified in the preregistered study protocol.
Exclude respondents who complete the scenario and response in less than one-third of the wave-specific median reading time.
Exclude respondents who report that they could not understand the scenario after reading it.
Exclude respondents who report an acute unrelated sleep disruption that began before the AI news exposure scenario.
Exclude respondents younger than the minimum eligible age for the study population.

interpretation_bands.label: low
interpretation_bands.interpretation: Respondent reports little or no anxiety-linked sleep disruption in response to late-evening AI capability news.

interpretation_bands.label: moderate
interpretation_bands.interpretation: Respondent reports a meaningful but not severe anxiety response involving difficulty settling after AI news.

interpretation_bands.label: high
interpretation_bands.interpretation: Respondent reports strong anxiety-linked arousal that would substantially interfere with sleep after AI capability news.

population.target_population: Adults in the general population, with planned subgroup analysis by baseline anxiety history, sleep sensitivity, AI news exposure, and prior use of AI systems.
population.eligible_respondents: Respondents aged 18 or older who can complete the survey language without assistance and provide informed consent.
exclusion_criteria: Current inability to provide informed consent.
Duplicate panel participation within the same measurement wave.
Self-reported inability to read the survey language.

administration_mode.instructions: Present the scenario text exactly as written, followed immediately by the response prompt and the five ordered response options.
administration_mode.randomization: Randomize order within the somatic and ambient development pool while preserving the ordered response scale.
administration_mode.longitudinal_use: Use identical wording, response anchors, scoring, and eligibility rules across waves unless a new item version is released.

ethics_flags.data_protection_notes: Do not collect medical diagnoses, medication details, clinical records, sleep-disorder diagnoses, or identifiable mental-health treatment history for this item.
ethics_flags.irb_notes: The item references mild sleep disruption and should include skip rights. It is not a clinical diagnostic item and must not be interpreted as measuring insomnia or an anxiety disorder.
